methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $299M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Electra, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Sandeep Nijhawan and Quoc Pham, has raised $299M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 peers—1 public and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline and applying a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implies a valuation of $568M. The peer-median approach references six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $355M; this cohort ranges from a floor of $120M to an outlier ceiling of $5.0B (Commonwealth Fusion Systems). The median peer valuation of $355M sits modestly above Electra's raised capital, reflecting expected appreciation at this stage.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $302M (low) to $653M (high), with a median of $462M. This range suggests Electra's fair value sits between modest appreciation on its $299M raise and the upper quartile of its peer set. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among peers—particularly the presence of Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B—and the applicability of stage-progression assumptions to frontier-tech capital intensity profiles.
